raw_user_request: Make me an app for shift handoffs.
concrete_goal: Build a browser-based shift handoff board for charge nurses who need to record active patients, unresolved tasks, priority alerts, and readiness for the incoming shift from the first screen.
success_criteria: Charge nurses can add, edit, complete, and filter patient handoff cards without leaving the first screen.
The interface exposes priority alerts, outstanding task counts, and shift readiness in terminology suited to a clinical handoff workflow.
Desktop and mobile layouts preserve readable patient cards, controls, and priority indicators without overlap or clipped text.
Empty, loading, error, validation, disabled, and saved-success states are represented or deliberately simulated.
scope_in: Single-page handoff board UI and local client-side state for patient handoff records.
Seeded domain data for active patients, tasks, alerts, and readiness states.
Responsive controls, accessible form labels, keyboard focus, validation messages, and state views.
scope_out: Real patient health information, authentication, audit logging, integrations, paging systems, or production deployment.
Backend persistence beyond local browser state or static seed data.
assumptions: The prototype uses fictional patient records and must not request or store real protected health information.
Local browser state is acceptable for the first usable version.
The incoming shift needs a scan-first work surface rather than a marketing landing page.
implementation_constraints: Do not add external services, production deployment, or paid APIs.
Do not use real patient data or examples that resemble actual protected health information.
Keep the primary workflow available on the first screen.

target_users: Charge nurses and shift leads preparing an incoming team for patient status, unresolved care tasks, and immediate safety priorities.
product_brief.core_problem: Incoming clinical staff need a concise, reliable view of active patient handoff details so urgent alerts and incomplete care tasks are not lost between shifts.
primary_workflows: Create or update a fictional patient handoff card with room, acuity, assigned nurse, alerts, tasks, and readiness status.
Filter the board by priority, unresolved tasks, and readiness so the incoming shift can focus on unsafe or incomplete handoffs.
Review empty, invalid, saved, and error states while preserving a readable board on desktop and mobile.
domain_assumptions: Use clinical handoff vocabulary such as room, acuity, alert, task, readiness, and assigned nurse while avoiding real medical advice.
High-priority alerts and overdue tasks must be visible before secondary details.
content_data_model_assumptions: Primary entity: handoff record with fields id, room, fictional patient initials, acuity, assigned nurse, alert summary, task list, readiness status, updated time, and notes.
Task records include label, owner, due window, and status values open or complete; readiness states include ready, needs review, and critical.
UI states include first-run empty board, loading seed data, save success, validation errors for missing room or task label, disabled save while invalid, and recoverable local state error.
non_goals: No PHI handling, clinical decision support, external integrations, analytics, staff scheduling, or admin configuration.

ux_workflow_completeness: Primary add/edit/filter handoff workflow is usable from the first screen.
visual_polish: Priority, readiness, and task hierarchy are visually distinct without generic placeholder cards.
responsive_desktop_mobile: Desktop board and mobile stacked layout are both inspected with concrete viewport evidence.
accessibility_basics: Controls have labels, visible focus, keyboard-reachable workflow, and sufficient contrast.
error_loading_empty_states: Empty, loading, error, validation, disabled, and saved-success states are represented.
text_overlap_layout_stability: Long room labels, nurse names, alert text, and action controls wrap without overlap or clipping.
domain_fit: Labels, sample data, and interactions fit fictional clinical shift handoff work.
evidence_requirements: Desktop visual inspection artifact or screenshot, mobile visual inspection artifact or screenshot, primary workflow behavior check, accessibility basics check, and state coverage check.

scenario_flows.name: Create a priority handoff
preconditions: The handoff board is loaded with fictional seed data.
steps: Open the add handoff form from the first screen.
Enter room, fictional patient initials, acuity, assigned nurse, alert summary, and one unresolved task.
Save the record and confirm it appears in the priority lane with updated task count.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The new fictional handoff is visible, marked with the selected priority/readiness state, and can be filtered immediately.
failure_or_edge_cases: Missing room or task label shows validation and disables save.
A simulated save error preserves draft values and shows recovery guidance.

scenario_flows.name: Filter unresolved handoffs
preconditions: At least one handoff has open tasks and at least one is ready.
steps: Open a handoff card details area.
Complete one task and clear the filter.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Counts, card visibility, and readiness labels update without hiding urgent alerts.
failure_or_edge_cases: Empty filter results show a first-run style empty state with a reset control.
Long alert text wraps on mobile without covering controls.

verification_matrix.build: Run the repository build command when present or document why no build command exists.
verification_matrix.lint: Run lint or equivalent static checks when present.
verification_matrix.test: Run automated tests when present, or add a focused behavior test if the project has a test harness.
verification_matrix.run: Start the local app or open the static artifact and report the URL or file path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Exercise create, filter, validation, empty, error, desktop, mobile, and keyboard/focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing the handoff board, filters, form, and detail card with no overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing stacked cards, reachable primary actions, and wrapped alert text.
Behavior evidence for creating a handoff, filtering unresolved tasks, completing a task, and observing updated counts.
Accessibility evidence covering labels, visible focus, keyboard operation, and contrast-sensitive priority indicators.
State coverage evidence for the handoff board empty, loading, error, saved-success, disabled, and validation states.

iteration_policy.threshold_rule: overall_completion_score must be at least quality_score_threshold.
iteration_policy.rework_rule: Any failed app-quality evidence creates concrete rework before acceptance.
stop_conditions: Review is accepted with all app evidence present.
A PHI, external cost, or production deployment blocker is discovered.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Implement the first-screen shift handoff board and stateful workflow.
Review app/product evidence for desktop, mobile, behavior, accessibility, and states.
